Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery Requests And To Deem Admissions
Off Calendar Aug-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JAMES A. MICHEL, Esq. (Tel: 415-239-4949) for petitioner;
Atty. ROBERT S. AARON, Esq. of Aaron & Wilson, LLP (Tel: 415-745-0071) for respondents.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER YANET CALLES' Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery Requests And To Deem Admissions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Petitioner's motion to compel is denied as moot as respondents have now provided code compliant responses. Petitioner's request for sanctions in the amount of $1,145.00 is granted. Counsel for respondents have represented that payment in this amount has been sent.

Counsel for petitioner to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at drobbins@tongrobbins.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
